title: Magnetic Effect of a Current — Medium

questions.question: A student measures the magnetic field strength at different distances from a straight wire. Which statement is correct?
options: The field is stronger closer to the wire
The field is stronger further from the wire
The field strength is the same at all distances
The field only exists at the surface of the wire
questions.explanation:
The strength of the magnetic field around a wire decreases as the distance from the wire increases.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: Which of the following changes will NOT increase the strength of the magnetic field produced by a solenoid?
options: Using a longer wire with the same number of turns
Increasing the magnitude of the current
Increasing the number of turns per unit length
Placing a soft iron core inside the solenoid
questions.explanation:
To increase strength, you must increase current, increase turns per unit length, or add a soft iron core. Simply using a longer wire without changing these factors does not increase the field.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: In a solenoid carrying a current, where is the magnetic field the strongest and most uniform?
options: Inside the centre of the coil
Near the outside surface of the wires
Exactly at the two ends of the coil
At a distance of $10\text{ cm}$ from the coil
questions.explanation:
The magnetic field is strongest and most uniform inside the centre of the solenoid where the field lines are parallel and closely spaced.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: A student uses a plotting compass to map the field of a solenoid. If the current flows clockwise when viewed from one end, what pole is created at that end?
options: South pole (S)
North pole (N)
No pole is created
The pole depends on the voltage
questions.explanation:
Using the right-hand grip rule for solenoids: if the fingers follow the clockwise current, the thumb points away, identifying that end as a South pole. (The 'S' can be drawn with arrows following the clockwise loop).

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the soft iron core in an electromagnet?
options: To concentrate the magnetic field lines
To provide electrical insulation for the coil
To reverse the direction of the current
To increase the resistance of the circuit
questions.explanation:
A soft iron core is used because it is easily magnetised and concentrates the magnetic field lines, making the electromagnet much stronger.

**Common mistake:** Review the method above and check units/signs.
